programme.desc: With NASA planning to make a manned return trip to the moon by 2024, how long until some giant corporation turns moon dust into a cosmic-sized payday?

programme.title: The Age of Robots
programme.desc: By the year 2050, three qarters of the world's population will live in urban areas. Looking at robotic systems being developed worlwide we can take a glimpse at the city life of the future.

programme.title: Future Human A.I.
programme.desc: A look to the future to see how advencements in A.I. are increasingly blurring the distinction between what is human and what is machine.

programme.title: Ocean Predators
programme.desc: In this episode, Kina invites the viewer into his world of researching and filming great white sharks.

programme.title: Ocean Predators
programme.desc: In cold, remote waters, sea lions perform an underwater ballet full of grace and brute strength.

programme.title: Diving with Sharks: The Ultimate Guide
programme.desc: Dive into the ocean with predatory sharks like the great white to learn what they gain from human encounters.

programme.title: Ocean Wild
programme.desc: Off the coast of the Philippines are some of the most amazing sea creatures in the world.

programme.title: Ocean Wild
programme.desc: With 17,000 islands. the Indonesia Archipelago is one of the most biodiverse places on earth.

programme.title: Ocean Wild
programme.desc: Japan's unique coastlines are home to some of the world's most diverse marine ecosystems.

programme.title: Ocean Wild
programme.desc: Thai marine wildlife is like no other. In this episode, discover how these creatures evolved.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Shear Strength
programme.desc: On his home turf in Canada, Mark and his team chase one of nature's deadliest forces, coming face to face with tornadoes' shear strength.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Landfall!
programme.desc: Despite being under the weather, Meteorologist and Storm Chaser Jaclyn Whittal anxiously experiences her first Category 4 Tropical Storm, as she and Mark encounter Hurricane Matthew in Florida.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Best of
programme.desc: From hailstorms to hurricanes and tornadoes, the Storm Hunters revisit their craziest chases and share some of the most amazing moments in extreme weather events.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Monster Hurricanes
programme.desc: It's exhilarating. It's heart-wrenching. Mark and other storm hunters dive head first into some of the most destructive and deadly hurricanes to hit the US mainland in this millennium.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Perilous Pursuit
programme.desc: Say storm chasing and people think destructive tornadoes and ferocious winds, but those are only a few of the dangerous elements storm hunters Mark and Jaclyn encounter when pursuing extreme weather.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | True North Strong
programme.desc: Canada's weather is complex and powerful! Mark and fellow storm hunters chase down avalanches in British Columbia's back country, twisters in Saskatchewan, and hurricane scale storms in Newfoundland. Enduring extreme weather is just an ordinary day for the True North Strong.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Peak Intensity
programme.desc: Mark hikes up Mount Washington, New Hampshire, the highest peak in the Northeastern US and battles one of the most dangerous mountains in the world at its peak intensity.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | 1000 Year Storm
programme.desc: A slow moving disaster engulfs a city under water. The Stormhunters find themselves knee deep in flood waters as historic amounts of rainfall threatens to wash them away.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Fires of Fort McMurray
programme.desc: Flames fill the sky. An entire city flees in terror. Firefighters bravely rush in. The world watches on in disbelief and terror.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Blizzard 2016
programme.desc: A white monster charges up the Eastern Seaboard, and 80 million citizens are in its path. Mark battles the forces about to overwhelm iconic, and heavily populated, places in America.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Lake of Fire
programme.desc: Adventurer George Kourounis' patience is tested as he encounters deadly wildlife and challenging elements while preparing to descend into Marum Volcano in the South Pacific.

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Polar Quest
programme.desc: Stormhunters Mark and Jaclyn visit Canada's Arctic to see how the inhabitants live. Jaclyn also hopes to achieve one of her lifelong goals to come face to face with a polar bear. Will she be able to cross this off her bucket list?

programme.title: Storm Hunters | Shifting Winds
programme.desc: Mark and Jaclyn make their annual trek to Tornado Alley and this time they are looking for more than severe thunderstorms and tornadoes. They also want answers as to whether Climate Change is causing changes to Tornado Alley.

programme.title: The Latest in Ideas
programme.desc: New, fresh ideas straight from the brightest minds of our time. 1) A comedian's take on how to save democracy | Jordan Klepper 2) How do gas masks actually work? | George Zaidan 3) Is technology our savior — or our slayer? | Ruha Benjamin 4) What it's like to be a parent in a war zone | Aala El-Khani 5) What does "wealth" mean to you? | Aisha Nyandoro

programme.title: Laugh and Learn
programme.desc: You won't just laugh out loud -- these speakers will make you think twice. 1) Are you human? | Ze Frank 2) The fascinating (and dangerous) places scientists aren't exploring | Ella Al-Shamahi 3) Comics that ask "what if?" | Randall Munroe 4) How to win at evolution and survive a mass extinction | Lauren Sallan 5) Why elephants never forget - Alex Gendler 6) The world's largest family reunion ... we're all invited! | AJ Jacobs

programme.title: Best of the Best
programme.desc: Our best, most popular TED content over the years. 1) See how the rest of the world lives, organized by income | Anna Rosling Rönnlund 2) The legend of Annapurna, Hindu goddess of nourishment - Antara Raychaudhuri and Iseult Gillespie 3) We can make COVID-19 the last pandemic | Bill Gates 4) How AI could save (not destroy) education | Sal Khan 5) The secret language of trees - Camille Defrenne and Suzanne Simard

programme.title: Procrastination Station
programme.desc: Fascinating ideas for when you should probably be doing something else. 1) What happens to people's donated eggs and sperm after they die? | Ellen Trachman 2) Could an orca give a TED Talk? | Karen Bakker 3) How statistics can be misleading - Mark Liddell 4) What a nun can teach a scientist about ecology | Victoria Gill 5) Who is the fastest creature in mythology? | Iseult Gillespie 6) Is it really that bad to marry my cousin? | Mona Chalabi

programme.title: The Future Ahead
programme.desc: Get an awe-inspiring look at the future that awaits with visionaries already predicting, embracing and innovating on the potential of tomorrow. 1) How CRISPR lets you edit DNA | Andrea M. Henle 2) SpaceX's supersized Starship rocket -- and the future of galactic exploration | Jennifer Heldmann 3) Underwater farms vs. climate change | Ayana Elizabeth Johnson and Megan Davis 4) The affordable, 3D-printed bionics of the future | Enzo Romero 5) The future of fashion -- made from mushrooms | Dan Widmaier 6) The promise of quantum computers | Matt Langione

programme.title: Lead the Way
programme.desc: What makes a great leader? Surprising, nuanced approaches on how to inspire and empower others to do their very best. (1) What it takes to be a great leader | Roselinde Torres (2) The historic women's suffrage march on Washington - Michelle Mehrtens (3) The human skills we need in an unpredictable world | Margaret Heffernan (4) How great leaders inspire action | Simon Sinek (5) Harvey Milk's radical vision of equality - Lillian Faderman

programme.title: Ocean Wonders
programme.desc: Dive into the unexplored world beneath the waves: the beautiful, fragile (and often mysterious) deeps of the ocean. 1) Our campaign to ban plastic bags in Bali | Melati and Isabel Wijsen 2) How do schools of fish swim in harmony? - Nathan S. Jacobs 3) An economic case for protecting the planet | Naoko Ishii 4) How much would it cost to buy the ocean? | Astrid J. Hsu 5) Could fish social networks help us save coral reefs? | Mike Gil 6) Save the oceans, feed the world! | Jackie Savitz
